humanEthnicities.name: Chelaxian
humanEthnicities.description: Pale skin, dark hair, sharp features. From the devil-bound empire of Cheliax.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Proud, disciplined, legalistic. Many serve or oppose House Thrune.

humanEthnicities.name: Garundi
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, tall and lean. From the sun-scorched lands of northern Garund.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Ancient civilization of scholars, tomb-builders, and pharaohs.

humanEthnicities.name: Keleshite
humanEthnicities.description: Olive to dark skin, dark eyes, aquiline features. From the vast Kelesh empire.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Master traders, horsemen, and storytellers. Wealth and hospitality are paramount.

humanEthnicities.name: Kellid
humanEthnicities.description: Tanned skin, strong build, scarred. Hardy nomads of the north.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Tribal warriors who distrust arcane magic. Fierce and resilient survivors.

humanEthnicities.name: Mwangi
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, diverse builds. From the vast Mwangi Expanse jungles and savannas.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Diverse cultures of the Expanse — city-builders, jungle tribes, and ancient kingdoms.

humanEthnicities.name: Bonuwat
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, wiry build. Seafaring people of western Garund's coastline.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Master sailors and fishermen who revere Gozreh and Desna. Navigators of the Arcadian Ocean.

humanEthnicities.name: Bekyar
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, tall and imposing. From the southern Mwangi Expanse.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Feared slavers and demon-worshippers of the southern jungles. Their city-states trade in lives.

humanEthnicities.name: Zenj
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, lean and agile. Indigenous people of the northern Mwangi Expanse.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Ancient tribal people with deep ties to the land and ancestor spirits. Guardians of the jungle.

humanEthnicities.name: Shoanti
humanEthnicities.description: Bronze skin, tall and muscular, tattooed. Tribal people of the Storval Plateau.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Proud clans (quahs) with a warrior culture. Ancestral enemies of Chelaxian colonists.

humanEthnicities.name: Taldan
humanEthnicities.description: Fair skin, brown or auburn hair. Refined and cultured, from the old empire.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Heirs of a fading empire. Value tradition, pageantry, and noble bloodlines.

humanEthnicities.name: Tian
humanEthnicities.description: East Asian features, diverse complexions. From Tian Xia across the Crown of the World.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Ancient civilizations with deep traditions of honor, martial arts, and scholarship.

humanEthnicities.name: Ulfen
humanEthnicities.description: Fair skin, blond or red hair, tall and powerful. Northern seafarers and raiders.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Bold raiders and explorers. Value strength, honor, and saga-worthy deeds.

humanEthnicities.name: Varisian
humanEthnicities.description: Olive skin, dark hair, colorful clothing. Traveling people of Varisia.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Nomadic storytellers, fortune-tellers, and wanderers. The Harrow deck is central to their culture.

humanEthnicities.name: Vudrani
humanEthnicities.description: Brown skin, dark hair, often adorned. From the vast subcontinent of Vudra.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Deeply philosophical people with rich traditions of martial arts and divine magic.

humanEthnicities.name: Jadwiga
humanEthnicities.description: Pale skin, light hair, cold eyes. Descended from Baba Yaga's bloodline.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Ruling class of frozen Irrisen. Each generation descends from one of Baba Yaga's daughters. Rule over enslaved Ulfen.

humanEthnicities.name: Varki
humanEthnicities.description: Dark skin, stocky build, adapted to extreme cold. Arctic hunters of the far north.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Nomadic hunters of the Crown of the World. Expert survivalists in the harshest conditions on Golarion.

humanEthnicities.name: Nidalese
humanEthnicities.description: Pale, gaunt features with dark circles under the eyes. Shadow-touched people of Nidal.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: People bound to Zon-Kuthon's shadow for millennia. Stoic, secretive, and accustomed to suffering.

humanEthnicities.name: Azlanti
humanEthnicities.description: Tall, athletic, dark hair, violet or deep blue eyes. Remnants of a lost empire.
humanEthnicities.culturalNotes: Pure Azlanti are nearly extinct since Earthfall destroyed their empire. Those with Azlanti blood are considered exceptional.

Dwarf.name: Janderhoff
Dwarf.description: Sky Citadel of the Mindspin Mountains. Trade hub between dwarves and Varisians.

Dwarf.name: Highhelm
Dwarf.description: Greatest of the Sky Citadels, seat of dwarven culture and craftsmanship.

Dwarf.name: Kraggodan
Dwarf.description: Fortress-city holding the line against Molthuni expansion.

Dwarf.name: Surface-Dweller
Dwarf.description: Raised among humans in a surface settlement.

Elf.name: Kyonin
Elf.description: The elven homeland, an ancient forest kingdom.

Elf.name: Mierani Forest
Elf.description: Elves who remained during the exodus, now returned to this wild forest.

Elf.name: Ekujae
Elf.description: Jungle elves who never left Golarion. Fierce guardians of the Expanse.

Elf.name: Forlorn
Elf.description: Raised among shorter-lived races. Often melancholic but worldly.

Gnome.name: Brastlewark
Gnome.description: The largest gnome settlement, a chaotic city of inventors and tinkerers.

Gnome.name: Wanderer
Gnome.description: Constantly roaming to stave off the Bleaching through new experiences.

Gnome.name: First World Touched
Gnome.description: Stronger connection to the fey realm. More eccentric than most.

Half-Elf.name: Absalom
Half-Elf.description: The great city where all races mix. Half-elves find acceptance here.

Half-Elf.name: Varisian
Half-Elf.description: Born of elven and Varisian parents. Common in Sandpoint and Magnimar.

Half-Elf.name: Kyonin-Raised
Half-Elf.description: Raised among elves, often struggling to fit in with their shorter-lived side.

Half-Orc.name: Hold of Belkzen
Half-Orc.description: The orcish homeland. Those who leave often do so to escape brutality.

Half-Orc.name: Urgir
Half-Orc.description: The orc capital, a conquered dwarven Sky Citadel.

Half-Orc.name: Magnimar
Half-Orc.description: The cosmopolitan city accepts half-orcs more than most.

Half-Orc.name: Frontlander
Half-Orc.description: Raised in human borderlands, caught between two worlds.

Halfling.name: Cheliax (Freed)
Halfling.description: Escaped or freed from slavery in devil-bound Cheliax.

Halfling.name: Andoran
Halfling.description: Free citizens of the democratic nation. Proud abolitionists.

Halfling.name: Varisian Caravan
Halfling.description: Traveling with Varisian caravans as guides, cooks, or entertainers.

Halfling.name: Riverfolk
Halfling.description: Living along rivers and waterways, skilled boaters and fishers.

Aasimar.name: Absalom
Aasimar.description: The cosmopolitan city where celestial-blooded are most accepted.

Aasimar.name: Lastwall
Aasimar.description: Crusader nation where aasimars are revered as blessed warriors against evil.

Aasimar.name: Mendev
Aasimar.description: Born near the Worldwound, many aasimars are called to the crusade.

Aasimar.name: Unknown Heritage
Aasimar.description: Celestial blood manifested unexpectedly. Raised unaware of their heritage.

Tiefling.name: Cheliax
Tiefling.description: Common in devil-bound Cheliax where fiendish pacts run deep.

Tiefling.name: Worldwound Refugee
Tiefling.description: Born near the demonic rift. Distrusted despite fighting for good.

Tiefling.name: Absalom Outcast
Tiefling.description: Scraping by in the great city's underclass, mistrusted by many.

Tiefling.name: Nidal
Tiefling.description: Shadow-touched Nidal tolerates fiendish bloodlines more than most nations.

Orc.name: Hold of Belkzen
Orc.description: The savage orcish homeland of warring tribes.

Orc.name: Urgir
Orc.description: The orc capital built within a conquered dwarven Sky Citadel.

Orc.name: Darklands Raider
Orc.description: From orc tribes in the upper Darklands who raid the surface.

Orc.name: Exiled
Orc.description: Cast out from the tribe, now surviving among other races.

Goblin.name: Varisia
Goblin.description: Countless goblin tribes infest Varisia's hinterlands and coast.

Goblin.name: Isger
Goblin.description: Goblinblood Wars scarred this land. Goblins remain a persistent threat.

Goblin.name: Sandpoint Hinterlands
Goblin.description: The notorious goblin tribes near the Lost Coast — Thistletop, Birdcruncher, and more.

Goblin.name: Urbanized
Goblin.description: A rare goblin raised among other races, struggling with civilized ways.

Kobold.name: Darklands Warren
Kobold.description: From the vast kobold warrens that honeycomb the upper Darklands.

Kobold.name: Dragon-Sworn
Kobold.description: Served a true dragon as minion, now free to pursue own destiny.

Kobold.name: Varisian Foothills
Kobold.description: From mining colonies in the Mindspin or Kodar Mountains.

Kobold.name: Outcast
Kobold.description: Exiled from the tribe for being too clever, too kind, or too curious.

Catfolk.name: Southern Garund
Catfolk.description: From the warm grasslands and jungles south of the Mwangi Expanse.

Catfolk.name: Qadira Trader
Catfolk.description: Catfolk merchant traveling the trade routes of the east.

Catfolk.name: Wandering Pride
Catfolk.description: Part of a nomadic catfolk pride roaming between settlements.

Kitsune.name: Tian Xia
Kitsune.description: The ancestral homeland of kitsune, where fox-folk are part of legend.

Kitsune.name: Minkai
Kitsune.description: From the empire of Minkai where kitsune live in disguise among humans.

Kitsune.name: Avistan Exile
Kitsune.description: Kitsune who crossed the Crown of the World, living hidden in human cities.

Tengu.name: Tian Xia
Tengu.description: Tengu are widespread in Tian Xia, often as wandering swordsmen.

Tengu.name: Absalom
Tengu.description: A growing tengu community thrives in the great city.

Tengu.name: Magnimar
Tengu.description: Tengu who settled in the cosmopolitan City of Monuments.

Tengu.name: Riddleport
Tengu.description: Tengu are common in the pirate haven, serving as sailors and thieves.

Ratfolk.name: Darklands Trader
Ratfolk.description: Ratfolk merchant clans that trade between surface and Darklands.

Ratfolk.name: Katapesh
Ratfolk.description: Thriving in the great markets where anything can be bought.

Ratfolk.name: Absalom Warrens
Ratfolk.description: Living in the under-city of Absalom, running trade networks.

Nagaji.name: Nagajor
Nagaji.description: The naga kingdom where nagaji serve as the chosen people of the nagas.

Nagaji.name: Tian Xia Wanderer
Nagaji.description: Nagaji who left their naga masters to see the wider world.

Nagaji.name: Exiled
Nagaji.description: Cast out from Nagajor or freed from naga service.

Changeling.name: Irrisen
Changeling.description: Daughter of a winter hag from the frozen land of witches.

Changeling.name: Varisia
Changeling.description: Born to an annis or green hag of the wilds, raised by humans.

Changeling.name: Ustalav
Changeling.description: From the haunted land where hags are frighteningly common.

Changeling.name: Unknown Mother
Changeling.description: Raised unaware of hag heritage until the Call begins.

Dhampir.name: Ustalav
Dhampir.description: Gothic horror land where vampires are a persistent plague.

Dhampir.name: Nidal
Dhampir.description: The shadow nation tolerates the undead-touched.

Dhampir.name: Geb
Dhampir.description: The undead nation where dhampirs are practically nobility.

Dhampir.name: Hidden Birth
Dhampir.description: Born to a vampire's victim, raised unaware of their true nature.

Fetchling.name: Shadow Plane
Fetchling.description: Born in the Shadow Plane, where fetchlings have lived for generations.

Fetchling.name: Nidal
Fetchling.description: Shadow-touched Nidal has strong connections to the Shadow Plane.

Fetchling.name: Absalom Exile
Fetchling.description: Fetchlings who crossed over and now dwell in the great city.

Ifrit.name: Qadira
Ifrit.description: Fire-blooded ifrit are most common in the land of genies.

Ifrit.name: Katapesh
Ifrit.description: The markets attract all manner of genie-touched.

Ifrit.name: Absalom
Ifrit.description: The cosmopolitan city where any heritage is possible.

Oread.name: Janderhoff
Oread.description: Earth-blooded often drawn to dwarven mountain communities.

Oread.name: Five Kings Mountains
Oread.description: Living among dwarves who accept earth-touched kin.

Oread.name: Osirion
Oread.description: Desert oreads shaped by sand and ancient stone.

Sylph.name: Varisia
Sylph.description: Air-touched born in the windswept frontier lands.

Sylph.name: Lands of the Linnorm Kings
Sylph.description: Born amid the northern gales and icy winds.

Sylph.name: Absalom
Sylph.description: Sylphs drawn to the cosmopolitan city.

Undine.name: Absalom
Undine.description: Water-touched born in the great port city.

Undine.name: The Shackles
Undine.description: Undines among the pirate isles, valued as sailors.

Undine.name: River Kingdoms
Undine.description: Living along the many rivers that define this chaotic region.

Duergar.name: Nar-Voth
Duergar.description: Gray dwarves of the upper Darklands, bitter enemies of surface dwarves.

Duergar.name: Hagegraf
Duergar.description: The great duergar city-fortress in the Darklands.

Duergar.name: Surface Exile
Duergar.description: Rare duergar who left the Darklands for the blinding surface world.

Svirfneblin.name: Nar-Voth
Svirfneblin.description: Deep gnome warrens in the upper Darklands, hidden from drow.

Svirfneblin.name: Andoran Depths
Svirfneblin.description: Svirfneblin communities beneath the free nation of Andoran.

Svirfneblin.name: Surface Refugee
Svirfneblin.description: Fled the Darklands after their warren was overrun.

Wayang.name: Shadow Plane
Wayang.description: Originally from the Shadow Plane, adapted to Material Plane life.

Wayang.name: Tian Xia
Wayang.description: Wayang communities in the distant east.

Wayang.name: Wanderer
Wayang.description: A wayang far from home, seeking new shadows to explore.

homelands.name: Absalom
homelands.description: City at the Center of the World

homelands.name: Andoran
homelands.description: Birthplace of Freedom

homelands.name: Cheliax
homelands.description: Devil-bound empire of law and infernal contracts

homelands.name: Galt
homelands.description: Perpetual revolution and the soul-trapping Final Blades

homelands.name: Geb
homelands.description: Undead nation ruled by a ghostly necromancer

homelands.name: Hermea
homelands.description: Gold dragon's utopian island experiment

homelands.name: Irrisen
homelands.description: Frozen queendom ruled by Baba Yaga's descendants

homelands.name: Isger
homelands.description: Chelish vassal state scarred by the Goblinblood Wars

homelands.name: Katapesh
homelands.description: Markets of the impossible, where anything can be bought

homelands.name: Kyonin
homelands.description: Ancient elven kingdom

homelands.name: Lands of the Linnorm Kings
homelands.description: Viking kingdoms of the frozen north

homelands.name: Lastwall
homelands.description: Crusader nation guarding against the Whispering Tyrant

homelands.name: Magnimar
homelands.description: City of Monuments on the Lost Coast

homelands.name: Mendev
homelands.description: Crusader nation bordering the Worldwound

homelands.name: Molthune
homelands.description: Expansionist military nation at war with Nirmathas

homelands.name: Mwangi Expanse
homelands.description: Vast jungles and savannas of central Garund

homelands.name: Nex
homelands.description: Arcane nation founded by the archmage Nex

homelands.name: Nidal
homelands.description: Shadow-cursed theocracy of Zon-Kuthon

homelands.name: Nirmathas
homelands.description: Forest nation of guerrilla freedom fighters

homelands.name: Numeria
homelands.description: Savage land of fallen starships and alien technology

homelands.name: Osirion
homelands.description: Land of pharaohs and ancient tombs

homelands.name: Qadira
homelands.description: Gateway to the east, land of genies and trade

homelands.name: Razmiran
homelands.description: Theocracy ruled by a false living god

homelands.name: Riddleport
homelands.description: Pirate haven beneath the Cyphergate

homelands.name: River Kingdoms
homelands.description: Lawless patchwork of petty kingdoms and bandit lords

homelands.name: Sandpoint
homelands.description: Sleepy coastal town on the Lost Coast

homelands.name: The Shackles
homelands.description: Pirate isles ruled by the Hurricane King

homelands.name: Taldor
homelands.description: Crumbling empire of decadent nobility

homelands.name: The Worldwound
homelands.description: Demon-infested rift torn in the fabric of reality

homelands.name: Thuvia
homelands.description: Desert land famous for its sun orchid elixir

homelands.name: Ustalav
homelands.description: Gothic horror land haunted by the undead

homelands.name: Varisia
homelands.description: Frontier land of ancient Thassilonian ruins

homelands.name: Vudra
homelands.description: Vast subcontinent of a hundred kingdoms

homelands.name: Tian Xia
homelands.description: Dragon Empires of the distant east

homelands.name: Crown of the World
homelands.description: Frozen roof of the world connecting Avistan and Tian Xia

homelands.name: Darklands
homelands.description: The vast underworld beneath Golarion's surface

homelands.name: Hold of Belkzen
homelands.description: Savage orcish territory of warring tribes

homelands.name: Korvosa
homelands.description: Chelish-founded city of strict order and ancient curses
